title: Revert to Image-Based Questions and Options with Responsive Image Display

requirements.text: In the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo), revert the Question data model back to image-based fields: replace any plain-text question/option fields with `questionImageUrl: Text` and `optionImageUrls: [Text]` (array of 4 image URLs). Update all related backend functions — addQuestion, getAllQuestions, getTestQuestions — to use these image URL fields. Ensure the data model is consistent with the original image-based schema.

quotes: our question and options are image based so please add question and option image from gallery option

acceptanceCriteria: Question type has `questionImageUrl: Text` and `optionImageUrls: [Text]` fields (4 URLs)
addQuestion accepts questionImageUrl and optionImageUrls parameters
getAllQuestions and getTestQuestions return image URL fields correctly
Backend compiles and deploys without errors

requirements.text: Write a migration module (backend/migration.mo) that transforms any existing questions stored with plain-text fields to the image-URL-based schema (initializing image URL fields as empty strings if needed), so stable state is preserved across the canister upgrade.

quotes: our question and options are image based

acceptanceCriteria: migration.mo handles the schema change from text-based to image-URL-based questions
Existing question records are migrated without data loss
Canister upgrades without trapping on old state

requirements.text: Update the Admin Panel 'Add Question' modal (frontend/src/components/admin/AddQuestionModal.tsx) to use image URL input fields instead of plain text fields. The modal must contain: a text input for the question image URL, four text inputs labeled 'Option 1 Image URL' through 'Option 4 Image URL', a correct option selector (radio buttons), and a Submit button. On submit, call the addQuestion backend function with the image URL values. Show a preview thumbnail (small, e.g. max-h-32) below each URL input field so the admin can verify the image before submitting.

quotes: add question and option image from gallery option

acceptanceCriteria: Modal has a question image URL input with a live preview thumbnail
Modal has four option image URL inputs each with a live preview thumbnail
Radio buttons allow selecting the correct option (1–4)
Submitting calls addQuestion with image URL values
Modal closes on success and shows a success toast
Question gallery refreshes to show the new question

requirements.text: Update the Admin Panel Question Gallery (frontend/src/components/admin/QuestionGallery.tsx) to display questions and options as images instead of text. Each question card must render the question image prominently, and show all four option images in a 2×2 grid below it, with the correct answer option visually highlighted (e.g. green border). Image containers must be responsive: use `w-full object-contain` with a defined max height (e.g. max-h-48 for question, max-h-24 for options) so images scale to fit without overflow, and are clearly visible on both mobile and desktop.

quotes: images fixing folder are adjustable as per image size so that question and images see clearly to users

acceptanceCriteria: Each question card shows the question image with object-contain and a defined max height
Four option images are shown in a 2×2 grid per card
Correct answer option image is highlighted with a green border
Image containers are responsive and do not overflow on mobile or desktop
Images are clearly visible at all screen sizes

requirements.text: Update the QuestionDisplay component (frontend/src/components/QuestionDisplay.tsx) to render the question and answer options as images instead of text. Display the question image in a responsive container (w-full, object-contain, adjustable max height e.g. max-h-64 on mobile, max-h-80 on desktop) so it is clearly visible. Display each of the four option images as a selectable card in a 2-column grid on tablet/desktop and single column on mobile. Each option image container must use object-contain with a defined max height (e.g. max-h-40) so images are fully visible. The selected option card is highlighted with a navy border.

quotes: images fixing folder are adjustable as per image size so that question and images see clearly to users

acceptanceCriteria: Question image renders with object-contain in a responsive container
Question image max height is defined and adjustable, ensuring full visibility
Four option images render as selectable cards in a 2-column grid on tablet/desktop and 1-column on mobile
Option image containers use object-contain with defined max height
Selected option card shows a navy border highlight
No image overflow or clipping on any screen size

requirements.text: Update the Test Page answer review section (frontend/src/pages/Test.tsx) to render question and option images instead of text. For each reviewed question, display the question image in a responsive container (object-contain, max-h-64). Show all four option images as styled cards in a 2-column grid on desktop and single column on mobile, each with object-contain and max-h-40. Highlight the user's selected answer card with a blue border and the correct answer card with a green border.

quotes: our question and options are image based
images fixing folder are adjustable as per image size so that question and images see clearly to users

acceptanceCriteria: Answer review shows question images with object-contain in responsive containers
Option images are shown as cards in a 2-column grid on desktop, 1-column on mobile
User's selected answer is highlighted with a blue border
Correct answer is highlighted with a green border
Images do not overflow or get clipped at any screen size

constraints: Do not use text fields for questions or options — all question and option content must be stored and displayed as image URLs
Image containers must use object-contain (not object-cover) so full images are always visible without cropping
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Adding image upload/file-picker functionality — image URLs are entered manually
Changing the Navy Blue and white design theme
Modifying authentication or profile setup flow
Changing the leaderboard or results pages
